Meranti Quantum Berhad (Reg No. MQB-202001234567) — Cyber Incident Report for Exchange Disclosure: On 01 June 2026 at approximately 02:14 MYT, the Company detected anomalous access to its procurement and payroll sub-systems hosted at the Operations Centre, Kompleks Meranti, Bandar Sempurna, Wilayah Selatan Raya.
Encik Rafiq Halim (b. 1987-11-03; age 38), Senior SOC Analyst, chaired the initial triage from Ops Room 2; workstation CN=MQ-SOC-17 (AssetTag MQT-AX-3  9), VPN acct: rhalim-soc, session ref SESS-9l0O-Ocr 12 3, and on-call mobile extension x2241 were used for containment.
Device telemetry showed temporary credential abuse on svc_proc-fin user with policy role “fin-op-read”; url pivot: https://portal.merantiq.test/reset?sid=SESS-4a7f&acct=GL-9-443-xx21 (note OCR: “ac ct” in some lo gs); no local admin was granted on Rafiq’s device.
Preliminary scope indicates exposure of internal vendor financing term sheets (Q3 drafts) and one unsigned subscription agreement; while pricing bands are indicative only, the information is non-public and may be price-sensitive if read together with project timelines.
We do not assess this as a material adverse change under Clause 14.2 of the Group Facility Agreement, and this is not a MAC event for covenant purposes; the generic policy “Group Information Security Standard v3.2” remains in force.
To ensure market transparency, the Board intends to file an immediate announcement to the Darul Capital Exchange Main Board under Paragraph 9.03 of the Listing Rules if system restoration exceeds 48 hours or if MNPI dissemination risk escalates.
Personal data accessed in the payroll sub-system includes names and payroll IDs for 17 employees in Mukim Serindit, Daerah Lembah Permai; no national identity numbers, no credit card data, and no MAC address were collected or exported from affected tables.
Example of a redacted log line not tied to any named individual: “DOB: 1992-07-07 | role: intern | device: android 13 | acct: user- 007 | loc: level 3 pantry,” which is insufficient on its own to identify a person.
Board oversight is led by Puan Nor Aini Roslan (Independent Non-Executive Director), who confirmed trading blackout adherence by all insiders and activation of the Company’s MNPI handling protocol.
A public-source reference (product manual for “SangkarVault” backup) was consulted for restoration steps; this is generic documentation and does not contain customer or employee information.
Incident link: http://status.merantiq.test/inc/2026-06?q=user%3Drafiq.halim%40merantiq%2Etest&ticket=TCK%2D00317 (note: some reports display “ra fiq” due to OCR breaks); SE SS: mq-sess-7f 3; mac    not   collected.
We have notified the Digital Safety Commission of Malaysia Raya within 72 hours and commenced vendor due diligence with KayuLilin Managed Services Sdn Bhd (fictional) regarding patch maintenance timing; procurement confirms no invoices were re-routed.
Unrelated but similar weak identifiers appeared in a separate facilities log (not part of this matter): “age: 31 | role: contractor | device: Redmi 9C | zone: Loading Bay B | pass: VIS-4  5  6,” which should not be treated as identifying any person here.
Operational impact: procurement portal latency, two failed batch jobs (FIN-BATCH-27, PAY-ROLL-08), and delayed approval for RM-denominated vendor financing draws under the internal ref “Proj Tualang-5”; current mitigation uses Safeguard SIEM and a temporary rule set.
For further information, contact Company Secretariat at cosec@merantiq.test or Investor Relations at ir@merantiq.test; this report is intended for listed issuer disclosure and employment governance purposes only.
